text: In this artificial, almost otherworldly space, the glow of colored lights through glass feels both inviting and alien. My senses are heightened—there is a feeling of anticipation and curiosity, as if genius or invention is about to emerge. The words "research" and "development" on the wall plant seeds of possibility, yet the electronic hum and stark lines create a slight disquiet. I am acutely aware of the duality: comfort in the shared pursuit of knowledge, and unease in the surveillance, the relentless observation that comes with being here. The weight of inquiry and expectation sits lightly upon me, urging me to reflect on the nature of progress and the boundaries we constantly push.
